title: An integrated, multistudy analysis of the safety of Ann Arbor strain live attenuated influenza vaccine in children aged 2-17 years.
ehr_evidence: The study is an integrated analysis of randomized controlled trials (RCTs), not electronic health records.
summary: An integrated analysis of multiple randomized controlled trials to evaluate the safety and reactogenicity of the Ann Arbor strain live attenuated influenza vaccine (LAIV) in children aged 2–17 years.
key_findings: LAIV recipients exhibited increased runny/stuffy nose, headache, and tiredness/decreased activity compared to placebo.
No evidence of an increase in any potential vaccine-related serious adverse events.
No evidence that lower respiratory illness or wheezing illness occurred at a higher rate in LAIV recipients.
study_design: Integrated analysis of randomized controlled trials
limitations: Integrated analysis of existing trials rather than a single prospective cohort
